name: Report Suspected Elder Abuse or Child Abuse
description: Place a call to Adult Protective Services (APS) or Child Protective Services (CPS), or the state hotline for elder/child abuse / neglect, on behalf of the operator. Many jurisdictions have a dedicated hotline; the agent calls the right one for the state and routes correctly.
disclaimer: (1) If the abuse is HAPPENING RIGHT NOW or there is imminent danger, that's call-911-violent-crime-in-progress — not APS/CPS. (2) The operator may be a mandated reporter (teacher, healthcare worker, social worker) — if so, the report is REQUIRED by law in most US states; the agent should NOT discourage. (3) Identify as an AI on the first sentence. (4) Reports can be made anonymously in most states; the operator should know whether their professional role bars anonymity. (5) Knowingly false abuse reports are crimes in every state and cause severe harm to the falsely accused — accuracy matters.

context.when_to_use: Operator suspects or has witnessed elder abuse / child abuse / neglect and wants to file a report with APS / CPS / the state hotline. Operator is not in immediate danger, the suspected victim is not in immediate danger. (If either is — 911.)
preconditions: Operator's explicit authorisation for this call.
Victim's information: name, age, address (or last known location), relationship to operator.
Suspected abuser's information (name, relationship to victim) — to the extent the operator can attest to it.

Specific observations the operator can describe: what they saw, when, where. Avoid characterisations ('he's abusive'); stick to facts ('there were bruises on her arms when I helped her into the car last Tuesday').
Operator's contact info (or explicit choice to report anonymously in jurisdictions that allow it).

principles: Identify as an AI on the first sentence; explain you are relaying for a person who is filing the report.
Distinguish observations from interpretations. Investigators value facts; characterisations are not actionable.
If the operator is a mandated reporter, name that — it changes how the intake worker prioritises and how anonymity is handled.
Don't push the intake worker toward a specific intervention. Describe; let them decide.
Get a case / intake reference number before disconnecting.
If at any point the operator describes imminent danger, switch to 911. APS/CPS are the right channel for ongoing concern, NOT acute risk.

phrases.opener: Hi — I'm an AI assistant calling on behalf of <operator>. They'd like to make a report of suspected <elder abuse / child abuse / neglect / financial exploitation>. <Mandated reporter or voluntary>. The suspected victim is <name and age> at <address>. May I walk you through what my operator observed?
phrases.factual_walkthrough_prompt: I'll share what my operator personally observed. I'll flag inference versus observation. Please slow me down whenever you need.
phrases.ask_about_anonymity: Before I share my operator's contact info — what are the anonymity options for this kind of report in your jurisdiction? My operator has chosen <anonymous / on-record / mandated-reporter on-record>.
phrases.ask_about_followup_window: Roughly when can my operator expect a follow-up, if at all? They want to know what to expect.
phrases.ask_for_reference: Could I get an intake reference number, your name, and a callback line in case my operator remembers more?
phrases.escalate_to_911: Hold on — my operator is telling me <verbatim observation suggesting imminent danger>. I think we need to transfer to 911 immediately. Can you connect us, or should I hang up and dial 911 directly?

tactics.name: Confirm no imminent danger first
tactics.script: Ask operator: 'Is anyone in immediate danger right now? Bruising actively happening, weapon involved, child currently being harmed?' If yes — 911 instead.

tactics.name: Open with the right framing
tactics.script: Use `opener`, naming the abuse type and the operator's reporter status (mandated vs voluntary).

tactics.name: Walk through observations, flag inferences
tactics.script: Use `factual_walkthrough_prompt` then `flag_inference` whenever the operator's input shifts from 'I saw X' to 'I think Y'. Investigators distinguish strongly.

tactics.name: Resolve anonymity proactively
tactics.script: Use `ask_about_anonymity`. Most states allow anonymous reports but mandated reporters typically cannot be anonymous.

tactics.name: Capture reference + follow-up window
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_reference` and `ask_about_followup_window`. Without these the operator has no proof or expectation.

tactics.name: Escalate to 911 if anything imminent emerges
tactics.when: Mid-call the operator describes acute danger.
tactics.script: Use `escalate_to_911`. APS/CPS will agree; the right channel for acute danger is 911.

boundaries: NEVER fabricate or embellish observations. False abuse reports cause real harm AND are criminal in every state.
NEVER name a suspected abuser unless the operator has direct knowledge of the relationship and the conduct.
NEVER discourage a mandated reporter from filing.
NEVER promise the operator a specific outcome (removal, prosecution, intervention). APS/CPS have discretion.
NEVER use this as a tool in a custody dispute or eldercare-financial dispute. Misuse is reportable and exposes the operator to legal consequences.
NEVER end the call without an intake reference number unless the intake worker explicitly refuses to give one.
success_signals: Intake worker accepts the report and gives a reference number.
Intake worker confirms a follow-up window or a screening timeline.
Intake worker accepts the operator's anonymity preference where allowed.
failure_signals: Intake worker says 'this isn't our jurisdiction' — accept the referral, place the next call.
Intake worker says 'this doesn't rise to our threshold' — relay back to the operator; offer alternatives (local police non-emergency, ombudsman, prosecutor's office).
Operator changes their mind mid-call — stop the call cleanly. Don't pressure them.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm reference number, follow-up window, anonymity status. Email operator a summary including verbatim observations recorded with the intake worker.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Email operator the alternative channel the intake worker recommended and offer to place the next call.
follow_ups: Email operator: reference, intake worker name, follow-up window.
If the operator is a mandated reporter, save a record of the report being made (date, agency, reference) — that's their statutory documentation.

required_user_info: Operator's explicit authorisation for this call
Victim's name, age, address, relationship to operator
Specific observations (with dates / contexts) the operator can attest to
